Where World Cup 2026 Players Come From and Where They Play

Felipe Costa • June 22, 2026

Every World Cup squad is a small map of global football labour: players are born into one football continent and employed in another. This post looks at the 48 confirmed squads for the 2026 tournament from two angles — first the aggregate flow between continents, then a single team at a time.

A note on grouping: each country is assigned to one of seven continents (Europe, Africa, N. America, S. America, W. Asia, E. Asia, Oceania) on a geographic basis. A few cross-boundary cases were judgement calls — Turkey, the South Caucasus, and Central Asia sit in W. Asia, while Russia and Cyprus sit in Europe. See the lookup table in the repo for the full mapping.

The big picture: continent-to-continent flows

The chord diagram below is directed. Each arc is a continent. A ribbon leaves the continent a player represents (their national team) and points, arrow-first, to the continent where their club is based. Thickness is the number of players. Ribbons that loop back to their own arc are players who stay on their home continent.

TODO: write the analysis of the aggregate flows (Africa to Europe, Europe staying home, Asia playing at home).

One team at a time

The aggregate hides how different individual squads are. Pick a national team below to see how its 26 players split across club countries, sorted from most to fewest. Green bars are players based at home; blue bars are players based abroad.

Plays at home Plays abroad
